title: INFRA: Migrate internal message queue client from legacy-mq-lib to async-mq-client v3
raw_text:
## Summary

Migrate all internal service-to-service message queue consumers and producers from the deprecated `legacy-mq-lib` (v1.x) to `async-mq-client` (v3.2). This is a pure infrastructure refactor with no user-visible behavior change.

## Background

The `legacy-mq-lib` library was internally developed circa 2019 and relies on synchronous blocking I/O. It is incompatible with the current Python 3.11+ async runtime and has been flagged for removal in the Q2 infrastructure dependency audit. `async-mq-client` v3.2 is the approved replacement; it offers equivalent functionality with native async/await support and improved connection pooling.

## Scope

Affected services (all internal, no external-facing APIs):
- notification-dispatch-worker
- audit-log-consumer
- ingest-pipeline-fanout
- dead-letter-requeue-cron

## What Does NOT Change

- No change to message schema or payload format
- No change to queue topology, topic names, or routing keys
- No change to retry logic or dead-letter queue behavior (same semantics, new implementation)
- No change to any user-facing functionality, API contracts, or UI behavior
- No change to SLAs or rate limits

## Testing

Unit tests updated to use the new client mock. Integration tests run against a local queue broker in CI. Load test confirmed < 2% throughput difference between old and new client under simulated peak traffic.

## Deployment

Blue/green deployment. Services will be restarted during the next scheduled maintenance window (off-hours). Zero expected downtime for dependent services.
